source_title: Antecedentes y fundamentos del Periodismo de datos
author: various

extracts.extract: Raw data is never sufficient by itself. For data to be useful and intelligible it must be processed, conceptualized, integrated into some kind of framework. The raw data must be inserted into some mental frame of reference that aids its interpretation and comprehensibility
extracts.notes: Meyer 1993 quote via Dader. Data without a conceptual frame is unusable.

extracts.extract: To locate new social facts of great journalistic relevance that allow knowledge previously unnoticed to be brought to light. To use methods developed in other disciplines in related fields to produce social knowledge. To apply a scientific perspective to newsworthy facts and phenomena. To locate new methods for identifying news stories. To build investigative models from those already developed in related disciplines.
extracts.notes: Five objectives of precision journalism analysis. Emphasis on borrowing methods from other disciplines.

extracts.extract: media organizations should create new specialized departments so that social scientists and journalists could share tasks. He showed the need to create mixed teams that could achieve a new type of journalistic information, that could discover stories that could only be obtained through extensive analysis.
extracts.notes: Meyer 1960s argument. Mixed teams of social scientists and journalists as prerequisite for data-driven stories.

extracts.extract: know or apply methods such as statistical analysis using specially prepared computer programs, the conduct or critique of sociological surveys and the searching of computerized databases
extracts.notes: Dader 2004 quote on required competencies for precision journalists: stats, survey critique, database querying.

extracts.extract: it is the same old investigative journalism (including methods of in-depth, precision, analytical journalism and what is known as 'computer-assisted'), to which some essential components must be added: Work is done with large volumes of open data, many of which come from closed formats, so in these cases a prior task of opening the data must be undertaken. It is team work: together with a data analyst, a programmer and an interactive visualization designer
extracts.notes: Crucianelli 2012 definition. Specifies three required team roles: analyst, programmer, visualization designer. Notes data often arrives in closed formats requiring liberation.

extracts.extract: data journalism involves the following steps: compile, clean, contextualize, combine and communicate the information. This process begins with a large amount of information that is gradually reduced throughout the process, leaving finally the information that we want to communicate or use.
extracts.chapter: 2.5. The Inverted Pyramid of Data Journalism
extracts.notes: Bradshaw inverted pyramid: compile-clean-contextualize-combine-communicate. Funnel from large volume to focused output.

extracts.extract: A journalist can obtain their database from different sources: 1. Databases obtained directly from a public or private company. Different organizations can offer us their data for thorough analysis in a reusable format. A great source of information today are public bodies, thanks to the Law of Transparency and Open Government. 2. Databases by browsing different Internet portals. Many websites offer information online that is susceptible to analysis. 3. Databases created through scraping. New technologies offer tools for extracting information from web pages in reusable format. 4. Databases from documents. Using a document, whether pdf or doc format, with conversion tools it is possible to obtain that information in a reusable format. 5. Self-produced databases. If we conduct an online survey or collect information through another system, it is possible to create a personal database.
extracts.notes: Five source categories: direct from orgs, online portals, scraping, document conversion, self-produced. Transparency law highlighted as key enabler.

extracts.extract: This technique called scraping or in Spanish 'rascar' (to scrape) is carried out using applications such as: Kimono Labs, Scraper Wiki and Outwit Hub, which automatically extract information from an Internet portal in .xml format for subsequent analysis.
extracts.notes: Scraping tools circa 2016. Kimono Labs now defunct; ScraperWiki renamed. Historically situated.

extracts.extract: Once we have the data, we need to trust it. The information in the database must be correct. To achieve this, human errors must be corrected, which sometimes arise when transferring data to a reusable format. In addition, we can eliminate data that is not useful for our investigation. Steps to carry out in data cleaning: Find and eliminate unwanted data in spreadsheets. Format the data appropriately according to the tools to be used for subsequent processing. Handle inconsistent data. Structure the data for easy handling.
extracts.notes: Four cleaning sub-steps: remove unwanted data, format appropriately, handle inconsistencies, structure for handling. Trust framed as outcome of cleaning.

extracts.extract: At this step we must interrogate the source and know how to interpret all the data we have. Questions to ask: Who collected this information? When was the data collected? For what purposes was it collected? Many times we will have fields in our database whose meaning we will not know at first glance. We must look up their meaning and know how to interpret them, to gain a broader view of the information we are seeking. Sometimes this step will lead us to collect more information, in order to better understand the data.
extracts.notes: Three provenance questions: who, when, why collected. Acknowledges that contextualizing may loop back to data collection.

extracts.extract: In order to display the information, combining two datasets is necessary. Although the information may be found in one source, it is necessary to combine it with another template, such as a map template to provide spatial visualization.
extracts.notes: Combining framed as joining data with a presentation template (e.g. map). Thin on method detail.

extracts.extract: This is the final point of a major effort to select and filter information. At this point the results are visualized through an infographic, a visualization or through a web application.
extracts.notes: Three output formats: infographic, visualization, web app. Presentation as culmination of filtering.

extracts.extract: Data journalism must perform its public service function of providing information by reporting the news objectively and based on data. This journalistic modality makes use of mathematics and some journalists go so far as to consider it a science, since given the same sources, another person must reach the same conclusion as the journalist.
extracts.notes: Reproducibility claim: same sources should yield same conclusions. Strong positivist framing.

extracts.extract: the Data Unit is made up of a multidisciplinary team of three people: Daniele Grasso, Jesús Escudero and Adrián Blanco. They also have the support of developers, designers and layout artists from the media outlet. Among the computer applications they use for their daily work, the following stand out: Microsoft Excel, Open Refine, Datawrapper, Infogram and Tableau Public. For more complex visualizations and applications they use D3, a JavaScript library.
extracts.notes: El Confidencial data unit toolstack circa 2016. Core team of 3 plus support staff. Two-tier viz: simple (Datawrapper/Infogram/Tableau) vs complex (D3).

extracts.extract: Among the tools they use to develop data journalism, the following stand out: Excel, OpenRefine, Tabula, Kimono Tableau, CartoDB and D3. As an independent section of the newspaper, they seek out their own topics, although they are permeable to current events and the topics being covered in the newsroom. Collaboration with the print newsroom is continuous.
extracts.notes: El Mundo data unit toolstack circa 2016. Adds Tabula and CartoDB. Notable: independent topic selection but continuous collaboration with print newsroom.

extracts.extract: A team of five journalists fact-checks statements made by politicians and public figures, showing what truth lies in them. The verification of information and journalistic documentation through data journalism has earned it the status of the leading program in its time slot.
extracts.notes: El Objetivo as TV fact-checking via data journalism. Verification as audience draw.

extracts.extract: talking about data journalism means referring to new techniques in the creation of journalistic information that requires specific knowledge and skills in the use of databases and the presentation (visualization) of information in new interfaces.
extracts.notes: Flores 2014 definition. Emphasizes dual competency: database skills + visualization skills.

extracts.extract: The goal was to establish a meeting point between computer scientists (Hackers) and journalists (Hacks), to exchange information about digital tools and analyze the contribution of programmers in areas such as handling large volumes of data, visualizations, etc.
extracts.notes: Hacks/Hackers model: structured knowledge exchange between technologists and journalists.

extracts.extract: Through rigorous investigation he compared insurance costs for different public real estate values in the same county. He also cross-referenced the names of insurance company executives who had contributed to school board campaigns with the school board directors
extracts.notes: Meyer 1959 method: compare costs across entities + cross-reference donor/beneficiary names. Foundational pattern for follow-the-money journalism.

extracts.extract: The laboratory is made up of a multidisciplinary team of journalists, web developers, designers and directors to get the most out of different digital narratives.
extracts.notes: RTVE Lab team: journalists + web devs + designers + directors. Lab model for multimedia/transmedia output.

extracts.extract: it is a discipline in which the power of computers is used to find, cross-check, visualize and combine information from various sources.
extracts.notes: Bradshaw definition. Four verbs: find, cross-check, visualize, combine. Cross-checking implies verification is built into the workflow.
